title: Classifying Web Content
text: cat.web thin domain wrapper around cat.stack adds: Automatic URL fetching — pass vector URLs input_data cat.web downloads page, strips boilerplate, classifies body text single call. Web-context prompt injection — source_domain, content_type, web_metadata arguments inject relevant context classification prompt (“news article nytimes.com…”). Everything else — supported models, output format, ensemble voting — identical cat.stack.

title: Classifying Web Content
Classifying Web Content
text: cat.web fetches URL polite User-Agent, extracts main content (dropping navigation, footers, comment sections), runs LLM classifier. output data.frame includes original URL, extracted body text (snippet ), one 0/1 column per category.
code: urls <- c(   "https://www.nytimes.com/2025/01/15/opinion/some-essay.html",   "https://www.nytimes.com/2025/01/16/us/breaking-news.html",   "https://www.nytimes.com/2025/01/17/technology/product-review.html" )  results <- classify(   categories    = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial/Review", "Other"),   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "nytimes.com",   content_type  = "news article",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" )

title: Classifying Web Content
text: already page content (perhaps scraping pipeline), skip fetch pass strings directly:
code: results <- classify(   categories    = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial", "Other"),   input_data    = df$article_text,   source_domain = "example.com",   content_type  = "blog post",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Web Content
text: source_domain, content_type, web_metadata arguments inject context model wouldn’t otherwise . matters short pages pages domain affects meaning (opinion nytimes.com vs. personal blog).
code: results <- classify(   categories    = c("Pro-policy", "Critical", "Neutral-explainer"),   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "vox.com",   content_type  = "explainer article",   web_metadata  = list(     section = "Policy",     audience = "general public",     style = "long-form explainer"   ),   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" )

title: Classifying Web Content
text: long pages, summarize first cut tokens improve focus:
code: summaries <- summarize(   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "nytimes.com",   content_type  = "news article",   format        = "bullets",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" )  results <- classify(   categories = c("Domestic", "International", "Business", "Other"),   input_data = summaries$summary,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Web Content
text: Respect robots.txt rate limits. cat.web doesn’t enforce crawl politeness — ’s . large jobs, add row_delay = 1 (higher) space requests. Validate extracted text. Boilerplate stripping isn’t perfect; sites model may end classifying cookie banner. Spot-check sample inputs scaling. Cache aggressively. Fetching URLs repeatedly development wastes bandwidth bumps rate limits. Save intermediate input_data one fetch re-use . Set timeout ’re hitting slow large pages — default (30s) short sites.

title: Classifying Web Content
text: Full Getting Started guide: vignette("getting-started", package = "cat.llm") Per-function reference: ?cat.web::classify, ?cat.web::extract, ?cat.web::explore, ?cat.web::summarize Companion R-package higher-precision retrieval: llm-web-research (Python currently).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Chris Soria. Author, maintainer.

CatLLM: Reproducible Python Ecosystem Generating, Assigning, Scoring Open-Ended Text, Images, Documents Across Research Domains (v3.0.0) [Software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19960067

what: cat.web — Web content classification for R
title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
text: Web content classification LLMs. domain wrapper around cat.stack adds automatic URL fetching web-context prompt injection (source domain, content type, metadata). cat.web wraps Python catweb package via reticulate.

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended once published): install.packages("cat.web", repos = "https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev")  # Or from a local clone: devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack") devtools::install("path/to/cat.web")  # Install the Python backend # pip install cat-web

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
code: library(cat.web)  urls <- c(   "https://example.com/article-1",   "https://example.com/article-2",   "https://example.com/article-3" )  results <- classify(   input_data    = urls,   categories    = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial"),   source_domain = "example.com",   content_type  = "blog post",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
code: results <- classify(   input_data = df$article_text,   categories = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
code: result <- extract(   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "example.com",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) print(result$top_categories)

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
code: results <- summarize(   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "nytimes.com",   content_type  = "news article",   format        = "bullets",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.web — Web content classification for R
text: MIT

what: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
title: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
text: Wraps Python catweb.classify() function. Accepts URLs (auto-fetched text) raw text strings. Injects web context (source domain, content type, metadata) classification prompt.

title: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
code: classify(   categories,   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   source_domain = NULL,   content_type = NULL,   web_metadata = NULL,   description = "",   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   timeout = 30L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   mode = "image",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_verification = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   thinking_budget = 0L,   example1 = NULL,   example2 = NULL,   example3 = NULL,   example4 = NULL,   example5 = NULL,   example6 = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 10L,   research_question = NULL,   models = NULL,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   use_json_schema = TRUE,   max_workers = NULL,   fail_strategy = "partial",   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   pdf_dpi = 150L,   auto_download = FALSE,   add_other = "prompt",   check_verbosity = TRUE )

title: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
text: categories character vector category names. input_data character vector / list / data.frame column URLs text strings. Default NULL. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. source_domain Character NULL. Source domain injected prompt context (e.g. "nytimes.com"). content_type Character NULL. Content type (e.g. "news article", "blog post"). web_metadata Named list NULL. Additional metadata injected prompt. description Character. Context description. Default "". filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. save_directory Character NULL. Output directory. timeout Integer. URL fetch timeout (seconds). Default 30L. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". mode Character. Processing mode. Default "image". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. safety Logical. Default FALSE. chain_of_verification Logical. Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. example1, example2, example3, example4, example5, example6 Optional -shot examples. model_source Character. Default "auto". max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 10L. research_question Character NULL. models List model specs ensemble mode. Default NULL. consensus_threshold Character numeric. Default "unanimous". use_json_schema Logical. Default TRUE. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. add_other Logical "prompt". Default "prompt". check_verbosity Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
text: data.frame classification results.

title: Classify web content using LLMs — classify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Classify a list of URLs (auto-fetched to text) results <- classify(   categories    = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial"),   input_data    = c("https://example.com/article-1",                     "https://example.com/article-2"),   source_domain = "example.com",   content_type  = "blog post",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" )  # Or classify raw text (no fetching) results <- classify(   categories = c("News", "Opinion", "Tutorial"),   input_data = df$article_text,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) } # }

what: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
title: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
text: Wraps Python catweb.explore() function. Returns every category string extracted every chunk across every iteration – duplicates intact.

title: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
code: explore(   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   source_domain = NULL,   content_type = NULL,   web_metadata = NULL,   description = "",   timeout = 30L,   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0 )

title: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
text: input_data character vector / list URLs text. Default NULL. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. source_domain Character NULL. Source domain context. content_type Character NULL. Content type context. web_metadata Named list NULL. Additional metadata. description Character. Default "". timeout Integer. URL fetch timeout (seconds). Default 30L. max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 12L. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. specificity Character. Default "broad". research_question Character NULL. filename Character NULL. model_source Character. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. focus Character NULL. chunk_delay Numeric. Default 0.0.

title: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
text: character vector every category string extracted.

title: Explore raw categories in web content — explore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ raw_cats <- explore(   input_data    = urls,   source_domain = "example.com",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini",   iterations    = 4L ) table(raw_cats) } # }

what: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
title: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
text: Wraps Python catweb.extract() function. Accepts URLs (auto-fetched) raw text. Returns normalised, deduplicated set categories.

title: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
code: extract(   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   source_domain = NULL,   content_type = NULL,   web_metadata = NULL,   description = "",   timeout = 30L,   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   mode = "text",   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0 )

title: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
text: input_data character vector / list URLs text. Default NULL. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. source_domain Character NULL. Source domain context. content_type Character NULL. Content type context. web_metadata Named list NULL. Additional metadata. description Character. Default "". timeout Integer. URL fetch timeout (seconds). Default 30L. max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 12L. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. specificity Character. Default "broad". research_question Character NULL. mode Character. Default "text". filename Character NULL. model_source Character. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. focus Character NULL. chunk_delay Numeric. Default 0.0.

title: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
text: named list counts_df, top_categories, raw_top_text.

title: Discover categories from web content using LLMs — extract
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- extract(   input_data    = c("https://example.com/page1",                     "https://example.com/page2"),   source_domain = "example.com",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" ) print(result$top_categories) } # }

what: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
title: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
text: Wraps Python catweb.summarize() function. Accepts URLs (auto-fetched) raw text. Web context (source domain, content type, metadata) injected summarization prompt.

title: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
code: summarize(   input_data = NULL,   source_domain = NULL,   content_type = NULL,   web_metadata = NULL,   timeout = 30L,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   instructions = "",   format = "paragraph",   max_length = NULL,   focus = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   model_source = "auto",   mode = "image",   input_mode = NULL,   input_type = "auto",   pdf_dpi = 150L,   creativity = NULL,   thinking_budget = 0L,   chain_of_thought = TRUE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   models = NULL,   max_workers = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   auto_download = FALSE,   safety = FALSE,   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   fail_strategy = "partial",   batch_mode = FALSE,   batch_poll_interval = 30,   batch_timeout = 86400 )

title: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
text: input_data Data summarize: URLs, text, data.frame column. source_domain Character NULL. Source domain context. content_type Character NULL. Content type context. web_metadata Named list NULL. Additional metadata. timeout Integer. URL fetch timeout (seconds). Default 30L. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. description Character. Default "". instructions Character. Specific instructions summary. Default "". format Character. Default "paragraph". max_length Integer NULL. Default NULL. focus Character NULL. Default NULL. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". model_source Character. Default "auto". mode Character. Default "image". input_mode Character NULL. Default NULL. input_type Character. Default "auto". pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. chain_of_thought Logical. Default TRUE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. filename Character NULL. save_directory Character NULL. models List model specs ensemble mode. Default NULL. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. parallel Logical NULL. Default NULL. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. safety Logical. Default FALSE. max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". batch_mode Logical. Default FALSE. batch_poll_interval Numeric. Default 30.0. batch_timeout Numeric. Default 86400.0.

title: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
text: data.frame summarization results.

title: Summarize web content using LLMs — summarize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ summaries <- summarize(   input_data    = c("https://example.com/article-1",                     "https://example.com/article-2"),   source_domain = "example.com",   content_type  = "news article",   format        = "bullets",   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini" ) } # }

title: cat.web 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catweb package. Domain wrapper around cat.stack adds automatic URL fetching web-context prompt injection (source domain, content type, metadata). Exports classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize().
